name: Applesauce
recipeIngredient: An average of 21 pounds is needed per canner load of 7 quarts.
An average of 13-1/2 pounds is needed per canner load of 9 pints.
A bushel weighs 48 pounds and yields 14 to 19 quarts of sauce -- an average of 3 pounds per quart.
recipeInstructions:
1. Select apples that are sweet, juicy, and crisp. For a tart flavor, add 1 to 2 pounds of tart apples to each 3 pounds of sweeter fruit.
2. Wash, peel, and core the apples.
3. If desired, slice the apples into water containing ascorbic acid to prevent browning. Drain the slices.
4. Place drained slices in an 8- to 10-quart pot.
5. Add 1/2 cup water. Stir occasionally until the apples are tender (5 to 20 minutes, depending on maturity and variety).
6. Press through a sieve or food mill, or skip pressing for chunk-style sauce.
7. Pack into jars without sugar if desired. If added, include 1/8 cup sugar per quart of sauce. Taste and add more sugar if preferred.
8. Reheat the sauce to boiling.
9. Fill jars with hot sauce, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
10. Adjust lids and process according to altitude using the provided tables.
Tables provide processing times for canning applesauce in boiling-water, dial-gauge, or weighted-gauge canners at various altitudes.
